title: Income Tax Notice Reply
description: Income tax notice reply by CA firm. Sections 142(1), 143(2), 148, 263 notices. Scrutiny assessment defence. Demand reduction. Virtual Auditor.
bodyText:
Income tax notices are issued by the Assessing Officer or CIT for various purposes: information requests (Section 142(1)), scrutiny assessment (Section 143(2)), reassessment (Section 148), penalty proceedings (Section 271/270A), and revision (Section 263). Each requires a different response strategy. Virtual Auditor handles all notice types — from simple information requests to complex scrutiny assessments — with CA-led response drafting, document compilation, and personal hearing representation. Quick Answer: Income Tax Notice Reply — Income tax notice reply by CA firm. Sections 142(1), 143(2), 148, 263 notices. Scrutiny assessment defence. Demand reduction. Virtual Auditor.
Income Tax Notice Reply is a service offered by Virtual Auditor, an AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm (IBBI/RV/03/2019/12333) led by CA V. Viswanathan (FCA, ACS, CFE, IBBI RV), specialising in income tax appeal representation before CIT(A) and ITAT, from offices in Chennai, Bangalore, and Mumbai since 2012.
Source: Income Tax Act 1961, IT Rules 1962, CBDT Circulars, Finance Act Official References: Income Tax Portal ↗ · IT Act Sections ↗
HEADING2: Regulatory Framework

Regulatory basis: Income Tax Act, 1961. Income Tax Rules, 1962. Finance Act (as amended). CBDT Circulars and Notifications.

HEADING3: Income Tax Notices — Section-wise Guide

Section | Purpose | Response Time |
142(1) | Information request | 15 days (extendable) |
143(2) | Scrutiny assessment | 30 days from notice |
148/148A | Reassessment (escaped income) | 30 days + objections |
156 | Demand notice | 30 days (pay or appeal) |
245 | Set-off of refund against demand | 30 days to object |
HEADING2: People Also Ask

HEADING3: When is income tax return due in India?

Non-audit cases: July 31. Audit cases (companies, firms requiring audit): October 31. Transfer pricing: November 30. Belated returns: December 31 with ₹5,000 late fee.

HEADING3: What is TDS and who must deduct it?

Tax Deducted at Source — every person making specified payments (salary, rent, professional fees, interest, contractor payments) must deduct TDS at prescribed rates and deposit with the government.

HEADING3: ⚡ How Virtual Auditor Delivers This Differently

Our assessment order parser extracts each addition with section reference, amount, and AO reasoning. It maps additions against our case law database (CIT(A)/ITAT/HC/SC precedents) and computes the economics: tax + interest + penalty saved per ground vs. probability of success.

HEADING3: Need Help With This?

Free 30-minute consultation with CA V. Viswanathan, FCA, ACS, CFE, IBBI RV. No obligation.

HEADING3: Step 1

HEADING3: Latest Regulatory Updates (FY 2025-26)
This page has been updated to reflect changes introduced in Budget 2025, recent notifications from CBDT, CBIC, MCA, SEBI, and RBI, and evolving compliance requirements for FY 2025-26. Virtual Auditor continuously monitors regulatory developments to ensure all advice and filings are current and compliant with the latest provisions.
HEADING2: Recent Engagement — How We Helped
Context: a salaried professional who received a demand notice under Section 143(1) for Rs 3.2 lakhs due to mismatch between Form 26AS and ITR.
Challenge: The assessee had claimed TDS credit for Rs 4.8 lakhs but Form 26AS showed only Rs 1.6 lakhs. The shortfall resulted in an automatic demand with interest under Section 234B/C. The individual was confused and worried about potential prosecution.
Our approach: We obtained TDS certificates from the employer and verified them against Form 26AS. The gap was caused by the employer filing a revised TDS return (26Q) that was not yet reflected in 26AS. We filed an online rectification request under Section 154 with supporting TDS certificates and the employer's revised 26Q acknowledgment.
Outcome: The demand was fully withdrawn within 45 days of rectification filing. No interest or penalty applied. We also helped the assessee set up a system to verify 26AS reconciliation before filing future returns.
This engagement illustrates Virtual Auditor's approach to income tax notice reply — combining regulatory expertise with practical execution to deliver results within the client's timeline.
HEADING2: When Is Income Tax Notice Reply Not Required?
Professional response to income tax notice may not be required when: (a) the notice is a routine intimation under Section 143(1) with a small adjustment that can be accepted, (b) the notice requires only basic information that you can provide without professional computation, (c) the notice is a reminder for return filing resolvable by filing the pending return, or (d) the demand is clearly erroneous and can be resolved through the online rectification facility. Notices under Sections 148 (reassessment), 143(2) (scrutiny), and 263 (revision) always warrant professional representation.
If you are unsure whether your situation requires income tax notice reply, contact us for a free preliminary assessment. We will advise you honestly — including telling you if you do not need our services.
HEADING2: Documents Required
The following documents are needed to initiate the income tax notice reply process:
PAN card of the assessee, Aadhaar card (linked with PAN), Form 16/16A from all deductors, bank statements for all accounts held during the year, investment proofs for deductions under Chapter VI-A, capital gains computation with purchase/sale documents, Form 26AS and Annual Information Statement (AIS) download, and TDS certificates for any payments received.
We provide a personalised document checklist after the initial consultation, tailored to your specific entity type and situation. Documents can be shared securely via email or our client portal.
HEADING2: What You Receive
Upon completion of the income tax notice reply engagement, you will receive: Completed tax return/form filing acknowledgment, detailed computation of tax liability with supporting schedules, reconciliation statements (26AS, AIS/TIS), advisory note on tax-saving opportunities identified, and a compliance calendar for future filing due dates.
All deliverables are reviewed by CA V. Viswanathan (FCA, ACS, CFE, IBBI RV) before release to ensure accuracy and regulatory compliance.

faqs.q: I received a Section 143(1) intimation. Is this a notice?
faqs.a: Section 143(1) is an automated processing intimation, not a notice. It shows the tax department computed result vs. your filed return. If there is a demand, you can either accept (pay) or file a rectification request (Section 154) if you disagree.

A reassessment notice — the AO believes income has escaped assessment. You must file a return in response. We evaluate the validity of the notice (was proper procedure followed?) and draft the response/return accordingly.

faqs.q: How quickly must I respond to an IT notice?
faqs.a: Varies by notice type. Section 142(1): typically 15-30 days. Section 143(2): as specified (usually 15-21 days). Section 148: 30 days to file return. Always respond within the deadline — non-response can lead to ex-parte assessment.

faqs.q: What types of IT notices can I receive?
faqs.a: Section 142(1): information/return filing. Section 143(2): scrutiny. Section 148/148A: reassessment. Section 156: demand. Section 245: set-off of refund. Each has different response timeline and implications.

faqs.q: What is the time limit to respond to IT notice?
faqs.a: Section 142(1): 15 days (extendable). Section 143(2): 30 days. Section 148A: 7-30 days. Non-response: assessment completed on best judgment basis, penalties imposed. Always respond within time even if requesting extension.

faqs.q: Can I respond to IT notices myself?
faqs.a: You can, but not recommended for 143(2) scrutiny or 148 reassessment. AO questions require strategic responses — admitting one point may create liability on another. Professional representation protects against self-incriminating responses.

Since 2020, most IT assessments are faceless (no physical meetings). National Faceless Assessment Centre issues notices and orders electronically. Response filed on e-portal. Video conferencing for hearings. Reduces corruption but requires precise written responses.

faqs.q: What happens if I ignore an IT notice?
faqs.a: Section 144: best judgment assessment (AO estimates income at high levels). Section 271(1)(b): penalty of ₹10,000 for non-compliance. Prosecution possible under Section 276D. Tax demand created without your input. Never ignore.
